overview.tagline: A classic small-town borough with train service, strong schools, and enduring community spirit
overview.description: The Borough of Waldwick is a charming, close-knit community in northwestern Bergen County that embodies the best of small-town New Jersey living. With NJ Transit rail service, well-regarded schools, and a compact, walkable layout, Waldwick offers residents a blend of suburban comfort and convenient commuting. The borough is home to a mix of Colonial, Cape Cod, and split-level homes on tree-lined streets, with an active volunteer community and a proud sense of local identity.
highlights: NJ Transit Main/Bergen County Line train station for NYC commuting
Tight-knit community with active civic organizations
Well-maintained borough parks and recreation programs
Affordable entry point for Bergen County with good schools
Walkable residential neighborhoods near the train station

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Waldwick's compact size and consistent housing stock make for relatively straightforward property assessments.
realEstate.recentTrends: Waldwick has experienced strong demand, particularly from young families attracted by the combination of NJ Transit train service, good schools, and relative affordability. Homes near the train station are especially desirable. Prices have risen steadily, and well-updated homes sell quickly.
neighborhoodNotes: Train station area — Walkable to commuter rail; popular with NYC commuters
Wyckoff Avenue section — Established homes along the main commercial street
Prospect Street area — Quiet residential streets with family homes
North Waldwick — Homes near the Ho-Ho-Kus border
South Waldwick — Properties near Midland Park border with easy Route 17 access

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by a contracted hauler.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Yard waste collected seasonally
Recycling bins available from the borough
Electronics recycling through Bergen County programs

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer services are provided through the Waldwick Water Department and municipal sewer system.
details: Waldwick operates its own water utility
Water sourced from local wells and regional supply
Quarterly billing for water and sewer service
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates commuter parking near the train station and enforces parking regulations.
details: Commuter parking permits available for the train station lot
Street parking in the downtown area
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Resident parking permits available for impacted zones

additionalServices.title: Waldwick Public Library
additionalServices.description: Community library offering books, digital resources, and programs for all ages.
details: Located at 19 East Prospect Street
Children's story time, teen events, and adult programs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Adult fitness classes and senior activities
Community events and seasonal celebrations
Facilities at borough parks

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Waldwick enforces noise regulations to maintain the peaceful character of its residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to daytime hours
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with height limits — 6 feet rear, 4 feet front
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Property exteriors must be maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Waldwick requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Barking dog complaints are enforced
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated on borough streets, especially near the train station.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Commuter parking lot requires a permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No parking of commercial vehicles on residential streets overnight
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental units must meet housing code standards
Certificate of Occupancy may be required at change of tenancy
Landlords must maintain properties to code
Fire safety requirements for multi-family dwellings
Short-term rentals subject to borough regulations

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permit may be required from the Borough Clerk
Limited number of sales per year per household
Signs must be removed after the sale
Sales restricted to designated hours

publicSchools.name: Waldwick High School
publicSchools.notes: Small high school known for personalized attention, strong academics, and competitive athletics

publicSchools.name: Crescent School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving all Waldwick students in grades 5-8

publicSchools.name: Traphagen School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving all Waldwick students in grades K-4

privateSchools.name: St. Luke's School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school serving the Waldwick and Ho-Ho-Kus community

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: NJ Transit train station with direct service toward NYC
Affordable entry point for Bergen County home ownership
Small-town feel with active community organizations
Strong sense of community and civic pride

commuteToNYC.duration: 55-70 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.notes: Waldwick has its own NJ Transit train station on the Main/Bergen County Line, providing direct service to Hoboken Terminal where commuters connect via PATH or ferry to Manhattan. Trains run frequently during rush hours. The total commute to Midtown Manhattan is approximately 55-70 minutes.

costOfLiving.summary: Waldwick offers a moderate cost of living for Bergen County, with home prices below the county median and a compact community that minimizes transportation costs.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 25% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Own NJ Transit train station for convenient NYC commuting
Affordable homes by Bergen County standards
Tight-knit community with strong civic engagement
K-12 school district entirely within the borough
Safe neighborhood streets and low crime
cons: Train commute requires transfer at Hoboken for Manhattan access
Limited dining and shopping within the borough
Small town with fewer amenities than larger communities
Property taxes remain significant by national standards
Compact lots — less yard space than neighboring towns

restaurants.name: Waldwick Station Deli
restaurants.description: Local deli near the train station offering sandwiches, salads, and breakfast items popular with commuters.

restaurants.name: Il Mulino
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant and pizzeria serving classic pasta dishes, brick-oven pizza, and family-style meals.

restaurants.name: Brian's Waffle House
restaurants.description: Beloved breakfast spot known for creative waffle combinations, omelets, and a friendly atmosphere.

restaurants.name: The Brickhouse
restaurants.description: Casual American restaurant and bar offering burgers, wings, and pub fare in a relaxed setting.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough's main park featuring sports fields, playground equipment, tennis courts, and a walking path around the perimeter.

parks.name: Pavilion Park
parks.description: Community park with a covered pavilion for events, playground, and open green space.

parks.name: Veterans Park
parks.description: Quiet memorial park honoring local veterans with walking paths and benches.

events.name: Waldwick Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Wyckoff Avenue featuring vendors, food, live music, and activities for all ages.
events.when: Spring or Fall, typically May or October

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual borough parade honoring veterans and service members, marching through the main streets.
events.when: Memorial Day, last Monday in May

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting & Festivities
events.description: Community holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, and a visit from Santa.

attractions.name: Downtown Waldwick
attractions.description: Small commercial district along Wyckoff Avenue and Franklin Turnpike with local shops, restaurants, and services.

attractions.name: Waldwick Train Station
attractions.description: Historic NJ Transit station serving the Main/Bergen County Line, a landmark of the borough's commuter community.

shoppingAreas: Wyckoff Avenue — Local shops, restaurants, and services
Franklin Turnpike — Businesses near the train station
Midland Park — Neighboring shops and dining
Westwood downtown — Larger shopping district nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 3rd Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 15 East Prospect Street, Waldwick, NJ 07463

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
